scope: SQLite surfaces across system-spec-kit, system-code-graph, system-skill-advisor + 027 deltas, oriented at Turso/libSQL migration

reuseCatalog.notes: WAL checkpoint pragma via .pragma() — Turso has no WAL checkpoint concept for remote replicas; this recovery step must be replaced or skipped.

reuseCatalog.signature: const reserve = db.transaction((): LeaseAcquireResult => { SELECT + INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE })
reuseCatalog.notes: Heartbeat-based lease acquisition in synchronous transaction; the entire single-writer lease model assumes local-file WAL locking — Turso's remote locking model invalidates this design.

reuseCatalog.signature: runInTransaction(database, () => { ensureSchema + readEdges + INSERT tombstones + DELETE edges })
reuseCatalog.notes: Core tombstone lifecycle: read edges → insert tombstones → delete edges in single transaction. Uses runInTransaction which must become async.

reuseCatalog.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_delete BEFORE DELETE ON mutation_ledger BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END
reuseCatalog.notes: DELETE trigger for append-only enforcement. Turso/libSQL lacks triggers — must be replaced with application-level guard.

reuseCatalog.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, 'mutation_ledger is append-only'); END
reuseCatalog.notes: BEFORE UPDATE/DELETE triggers enforce append-only immutability. Turso/libSQL lacks CREATE TRIGGER — this enforcement must move to application code.

reuseCatalog.signature: const tx = graphDb.getDb().transaction(() => { upsertFile(mtime=0); replaceNodes; replaceEdges; upsertFile(real_mtime); clearParseDiagnostic })
reuseCatalog.notes: Atomic per-file persistence in sync .transaction() — the core write-path unit for scans. Turso async transaction required; 4-statement atomic boundary is critical for crash safety.

reuseCatalog.signature: function runDaemonStateMutation<T>(dbPath, initialize, mutate, attempts=3): T with SQLITE_BUSY retry loop
reuseCatalog.notes: Generic SQLITE_BUSY retry wrapper opening a fresh DB per attempt; Turso has different error codes for contention — the retry predicate must be updated.
Explicit better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction wrapper; Turso transactions are async (await tx()). Entire function must become async.

reuseCatalog.signature: db.transaction(() => { for edge: insertEdge() }) with cache invalidation
reuseCatalog.notes: Nested transaction (calls insertEdge which also wraps in transaction); better-sqlite3 supports savepoints but Turso transaction API differs.

reuseCatalog.signature: database.transaction(() => { SELECT current + SELECT history + UPDATE + logWeightChange })
reuseCatalog.notes: Multi-statement transaction with fallback SELECT chain; synchronous transaction wrapper blocks Turso migration.
Synchronous transaction wrapping read-modify-write for atomicity; must become async for Turso.
Atomic DB swap relies on close/reopen cycle with WAL TRUNCATE checkpoint; Turso checkpoint semantics differ

integrationPoints.signature: db.transaction(() => { DELETE FROM vec_memories; INSERT INTO vec_memories(rowid, embedding) })
integrationPoints.notes: Transaction-wrapped vec0 virtual table writes (delete+insert per row) — blocks Turso migration entirely

integrationPoints.signature: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
integrationPoints.notes: FTS5 gate: determines whether SQLite FTS5 or in-memory BM25 is used; Turso migration must replace this with alternative lexical engine detection

integrationPoints.signature: ATTACH DATABASE ? AS legacy / DETACH DATABASE shard
integrationPoints.notes: ATTACH/DETACH used for 3-database migration (legacy→canonical→shard); Turso has no ATTACH — requires architectural redesign

integrationPoints.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE shard.vec_memories USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[dim]) + INSERT rowid/embedding
integrationPoints.notes: Creates vec0 virtual table in attached shard and copies rows — Turso has no vec0, no ATTACH, no loadExtension

integrationPoints.signature: database.exec(`INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('delete-all')`)
integrationPoints.notes: FTS5 'delete-all' command. Turso/libSQL lacks FTS5; this must be reimplemented with external search or alternative FTS.

integrationPoints.signature: database.exec(`INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('rebuild')`)
integrationPoints.notes: FTS5 'rebuild' command used in post-restore derived artifact rebuilds. FTS5 not available in Turso.

integrationPoints.signature: fs.renameSync + fs.copyFileSync file-swap restore with journal
integrationPoints.notes: v2 restore is entirely file-copy based (rename live→backup, copy snapshot→live); Turso remote DB cannot fs.copyFileSync — needs API-level restore

integrationPoints.signature: database.exec(`VACUUM active_vec INTO ${quoteSqlString(vecSnapshotPath)}`)
integrationPoints.notes: VACUUM INTO on attached database (active_vec). Turso lacks ATTACH and VACUUM INTO; vector shard snapshot needs complete reimplementation.

integrationPoints.signature: database.exec(`VACUUM main INTO ${quoteSqlString(mainSnapshotPath)}`)
integrationPoints.notes: VACUUM INTO is SQLite-specific; Turso/libSQL does not support it. Checkpoint creation must use alternative serialization (e.g., file copy or backup API).

integrationPoints.signature: SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
integrationPoints.notes: FTS5 virtual table existence probe; Turso lacks FTS5 — this check and all memory_fts consumers must be replaced or gated

integrationPoints.signature: Staging shard → atomic rename over active shard per reindex job
integrationPoints.notes: Reindex writes to staging shard then atomically renames. Uses vec0 virtual table creation inside shard. Migration must preserve atomic swap semantics.

integrationPoints.signature: Legacy profile DB → canonical + vector shard split with ATTACH DATABASE
integrationPoints.notes: Migration uses ATTACH DATABASE, vec0 virtual table recreation, and VACUUM. All three need Turso-compatible equivalents.

integrationPoints.signature: ATTACH DATABASE / DETACH DATABASE active_vec
integrationPoints.notes: ATTACH/DETACH for vector shard separation; Turso has no ATTACH DATABASE — shard architecture requires rework

integrationPoints.signature: database.pragma(shema.journal_mode/cache_size/mmap_size/temp_store/wal_autocheckpoint)
integrationPoints.notes: Five pragma calls on attached vector shard; Turso lacks .pragma() and ATTACH — entire shard initialization must be redesigned

integrationPoints.signature: db.exec('PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL; CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ...') — pragma embedded in exec string
integrationPoints.notes: PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL inside .exec() multi-statement; Turso libSQL may reject PRAGMA in batch/execute or handle differently.

integrationPoints.signature: new Database(dbPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true }); db.prepare('PRAGMA quick_check').get()
integrationPoints.notes: PRAGMA quick_check on readonly handle; Turso embedded may not support quick_check or fileMustExist option.

integrationPoints.signature: function shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine(database): boolean
integrationPoints.notes: Decision function for FTS5 vs in-memory BM25; Turso migration must redirect this to non-FTS5 lexical path

integrationPoints.signature: INSERT INTO memory_idempotency_receipts (...) VALUES (...) ON CONFLICT(receipt_key) DO UPDATE SET response_payload=excluded.response_payload, memory_id=excluded.memory_id, updated_at=datetime('now')
integrationPoints.notes: INSERT ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE (upsert) with datetime('now'). Turso supports upsert; verify .run() return shape matches better-sqlite3.

integrationPoints.signature: function init(database: Database.Database, vectorFn, graphFn): void
integrationPoints.notes: Binds better-sqlite3 Database handle; driver type import is the primary coupling surface for all hybrid search SQL.

integrationPoints.signature: handle.prepare('SELECT 1').get() — success means handle is still open
integrationPoints.notes: Probes DB handle liveness via prepare().get(); Turso async driver requires await — sync probe pattern breaks.

integrationPoints.signature: SELECT m.* FROM memory_index m JOIN active_memory_projection p ... WHERE LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ?
integrationPoints.notes: LIKE-based trigger phrase search with JOIN to active_memory_projection; standard SQL, Turso-compatible.

integrationPoints.signature: SELECT payload_hash, response_payload FROM memory_idempotency_receipts WHERE receipt_key = ?
integrationPoints.notes: Standard SELECT on memory_idempotency_receipts; Turso-compatible.

integrationPoints.signature: ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN learned_triggers TEXT DEFAULT '[]'
integrationPoints.notes: ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN DDL; supported by Turso but PRAGMA table_info introspection path differs in libSQL

integrationPoints.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET near_duplicate_of = ?, last_dedup_checked_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?
integrationPoints.notes: UPDATE with datetime('now') and JSON.stringify of hint. Compatible with Turso; low migration friction.

integrationPoints.signature: SELECT updated_at, last_dedup_checked_at FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?
integrationPoints.notes: Standard SELECT on memory_index; Turso-compatible.

integrationPoints.signature: ALTER TABLE RENAME TO / INSERT OR REPLACE FROM / DROP TABLE migration chain
integrationPoints.notes: Multi-step migration with RENAME/INSERT/DROP across attached schemas — Turso supports DDL but attached-schema prefixes must be reworked.

integrationPoints.signature: SELECT id, spec_folder, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id IN (?)
integrationPoints.notes: Standard SELECT on memory_index for BM25 scope resolution; standard SQL, Turso-compatible.

integrationPoints.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET near_duplicate_of = NULL, last_dedup_checked_at = NULL WHERE id = ?
integrationPoints.notes: Standard UPDATE with NULL assignments. Compatible with Turso; low migration friction.

integrationPoints.signature: ALTER TABLE memory_index DROP COLUMN learned_triggers
integrationPoints.notes: ALTER TABLE DROP COLUMN requires SQLite 3.35.0+; Turso/libSQL supports but verify version compatibility

integrationPoints.signature: UPDATE memory_index SET embedding_status = 'retry' WHERE id = ? AND embedding_status = 'pending' AND retry_count = ? AND ((? IS NULL AND last_retry_at IS NULL) OR last_retry_at = ?)
integrationPoints.notes: Optimistic locking via conditional UPDATE — standard SQL; Turso compatible.

conventions.notes: Pure filesystem path resolution for DB directory; no SQLite constructs — migration-neutral.
Pure markdown chunking logic; no SQLite constructs — migration-neutral.
Pure string normalization pipeline; no SQLite constructs — migration-neutral.
Pure in-memory scoring algorithm on already-retrieved results; no SQLite constructs — migration-neutral.

conventions.signature: no direct SQLite/driver coupling; StatediffTargetType names are table references
conventions.notes: Reconciliation engine is driver-agnostic; StatediffTargetType enum references table names (memory_index, embedding_cache, fts, bm25) but module itself has no SQL — migration-neutral.
WAL file triplet hardcoded; Turso uses different file layout — WAL/SHM files may not exist or behave differently with remote replicas.
Engine routing enum: 'sqlite' mode requires FTS5; Turso migration must add a new engine value or repurpose 'sqlite' to mean libSQL-compatible lexical
Multi-database ATTACH/DETACH for per-embedder shard isolation. Turso libSQL has different attach semantics and may not support cross-file attach.

conventions.signature: pragma journal_mode=WAL, busy_timeout=10000, foreign_keys=ON, cache_size, mmap_size, synchronous=NORMAL, wal_autocheckpoint, temp_store=MEMORY
conventions.notes: Eight pragma calls at startup; most are unsupported in Turso — need abstraction layer or removal
Quality-gap fallback explicitly names 'fts5' tier; Turso migration must rename or replace this tier identifier
Channel routing config includes 'fts' and 'bm25' channels; Turso migration must remap 'fts' channel to non-FTS5 lexical backend
Per-dimension blob tables (e.g. vec_768) for fallback storage. Naming convention must be preserved during migration.
Converts embeddings to Buffer for sqlite-vec storage format — Turso/vector-index replacement must handle identical binary serialization or provide compatible adapter.

conventions.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, ...); END
conventions.notes: CREATE TRIGGER for append-only enforcement. Turso supports triggers but syntax may differ; verify compatibility.

dependencies.notes: Direct better-sqlite3 driver import; Turso uses @libsql/client — every call site in this module couples to the synchronous better-sqlite3 API surface
Three .pragma() calls; libSQL lacks .pragma() method — must use db.exec('PRAGMA ...') or connection string params
loadExtension in reindex shard writer; Turso blocks extension loading — reindex cannot populate vec0 KNN table
Three .pragma() calls; libSQL has no .pragma() API — requires exec-based pragma or connection config
FTS5 optimize command after bulk deletes; Turso has no FTS5 — must remove or replace
loadExtension during shard migration; Turso blocks extension loading — migration logic cannot create vec0 tables
sqlite-vec scalar function used in every vector search query (also lines 403, 407); Turso has no vec_distance_cosine — must compute externally
FTS5 bm25() ranking function with per-column weights. No equivalent in Turso — requires external BM25 implementation.

dependencies.symbol: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5
dependencies.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text, ...)
dependencies.notes: FTS5 virtual table + 3 sync triggers; Turso lacks FTS5 — full-text search must use external engine or Turso's FTS alternative

dependencies.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text, content='memory_index', content_rowid='id')
dependencies.notes: FTS5 virtual table for BM25 full-text search. Turso lacks FTS5 — must use external search engine or Turso's native text search.
LoadExtension API for sqlite-vec. Turso (Rust) has no loadExtension — must reimplement vector search natively or via external service.
sqlite-vec distance function for KNN queries. Must be reimplemented if Turso lacks vec extension support.
FTS5 delete-all command; Turso/libSQL has no FTS5 — must replace with external search or Trigram/other index
VACUUM INTO on attached shard — Turso cannot attach databases or VACUUM INTO; snapshot strategy must change entirely
VACUUM INTO is a SQLite-specific snapshot mechanism; Turso/libSQL lacks this — must replace with serialize()/file-copy or Turso snapshot API

dependencies.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts USING fts5(...) + triggers + rebuild
dependencies.notes: Full FTS5 virtual table creation with content-sync triggers and rebuild; Turso has no FTS5 or loadExtension — blocks schema downgrade path
better-sqlite3 .pragma() API has no direct Turso equivalent; busy_timeout and foreign_keys must be set via PRAGMA statements or connection options; wal_checkpoint(FULL) may not be available.

dependencies.signature: fts5Bm25Search(db, query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived }) — uses bm25(memory_fts, 10.0, 5.0, 2.0, 1.0)
dependencies.notes: Delegates to FTS5 bm25() weighted scoring; Turso has no FTS5 — entire FTS channel must be reimplemented.

dependencies.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE vec_memories USING vec0(embedding FLOAT[dim])
dependencies.notes: Core sqlite-vec virtual table for KNN search. Turso lacks loadExtension and vec0 — blocks vector search entirely.
Three .pragma() calls (WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys) — Turso does not support .pragma() method; must use db.exec('PRAGMA ...') or configure via connection options.
PRAGMA database_list for attached shard detection — Turso does not support PRAGMA database_list; must use alternative shard discovery mechanism.
Three .pragma() calls (WAL, busy_timeout, foreign_keys) — same blocker as session-analytics-db; Turso requires alternative pragma configuration.

gaps.signature: db.pragma('busy_timeout = 5000')
gaps.notes: Turso/libSQL has no .pragma() method; busy_timeout must be set via connection config or PRAGMA SQL statement if supported

gaps.signature: ['code-graph.sqlite', 'code-graph.sqlite-wal', 'code-graph.sqlite-shm']
gaps.notes: WAL triplet file model (.sqlite + .sqlite-wal + .sqlite-shm); Turso uses remote replicas — all triplet copy/move/restore logic becomes obsolete

gaps.signature: db.pragma('integrity_check')
gaps.notes: integrity_check pragma is local SQLite; Turso has no embedded integrity check — recovery procedure CG-RP-001 must be redesigned

gaps.signature: db.prepare('DELETE FROM vec_memories WHERE rowid = ?').run(BigInt(id)); db.prepare('INSERT INTO vec_memories (rowid, embedding) VALUES (?, ?)').run(BigInt(id), embeddingBuffer)
gaps.notes: vec_memories is a vec0 virtual table (sqlite-vec) — Turso/libSQL has NO vector index support; this is a migration blocker requiring alternative vector storage.

gaps.signature: WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS (...UNION...)
gaps.notes: Recursive CTE with bidirectional UNION seed + UNION ALL recursive step traversing causal_edges up to MAX_HOPS(2). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE; must be rewritten as iterative app-side loop or stored procedure equivalent.

gaps.signature: WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS ... maxHops
gaps.notes: Core recursive CTE for graph traversal with bidirectional edges and relation-weighted scoring. Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must rewrite as iterative BFS in application code.

gaps.signature: WITH RECURSIVE causal_walk(origin_id, node_id, hop_distance, walk_score) AS (...)
gaps.notes: Recursive CTE for graph traversal (2-hop). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must be replaced with iterative application-side traversal or bounded UNION ALL.

gaps.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text)
gaps.notes: FTS5 virtual table for BM25 full-text search — Turso/libSQL lacks FTS5; entire FTS search path blocks migration

gaps.signature: database.exec('VACUUM main INTO ${quoteSqlString(mainSnapshotPath)}')
gaps.notes: VACUUM INTO is the core v2 checkpoint snapshot mechanism; Turso/libSQL lacks this — blocks checkpoint creation as-is

gaps.signature: WITH RECURSIVE dependents(child_path) AS (SELECT...UNION SELECT edge.child_path FROM dependency_edges edge JOIN dependents dep ON edge.parent_path = dep.child_path)
gaps.notes: Recursive CTE for transitive dependency closure. Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE; collectDependents() and wouldCreateCycle() both depend on this. Must rewrite as iterative app-side BFS.

gaps.signature: WITH RECURSIVE dependents(child_path) AS (...)
gaps.notes: Recursive CTE for transitive dependency closure (cycle detection + invalidation). Turso lacks WITH RECURSIVE — must rewrite as iterative BFS.

gaps.signature: ensureVecTableForDim(database, 768); ensureVecTableForDim(database, 1024) — creates vec_<dim> virtual tables via sqlite-vec
gaps.notes: sqlite-vec virtual tables (vec0 module) require loadExtension() which Turso does not support; this blocks vector embedding storage entirely.

gaps.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text, content='memory_index', content_rowid='id')
gaps.notes: FTS5 virtual table creation; Turso/libSQL has no FTS5 — blocks schema downgrade path entirely

gaps.symbol: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE memory_fts USING fts5
gaps.signature: CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts USING fts5(title, trigger_phrases, file_path, content_text, content='memory_index', content_rowid='id')
gaps.notes: FTS5 virtual table with content-sync. Turso has no FTS5 and no loadExtension; schema downgrade path must remove FTS entirely.

gaps.signature: CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_insert/update/delete AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE ON memory_index
gaps.notes: FTS5 content-sync triggers. All three triggers reference FTS5 commands; entire FTS sync layer must be replaced for Turso.

gaps.signature: fts5Bm25Search(db, query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived })
gaps.notes: Delegates to FTS5 bm25() ranking function; Turso/libSQL has no FTS5 — this entire search channel fails.

gaps.signature: CREATE TRIGGER memory_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON memory_index → INSERT INTO memory_fts
gaps.notes: FTS5 sync triggers (insert/update/delete) — dependent on FTS5 which Turso lacks; needs alternative sync mechanism

gaps.signature: INSERT INTO memory_fts(memory_fts) VALUES('delete-all')
gaps.notes: FTS5 'delete-all' command has no Turso/libSQL equivalent; blocks FTS table clearing during restore

gaps.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS memory_fts_insert AFTER INSERT ON memory_index BEGIN INSERT INTO memory_fts...
gaps.notes: FTS5 sync triggers for content='memory_index' external-content table; no Turso equivalent — blocks automatic FTS maintenance

gaps.signature: d.transaction(() => { ... }) — synchronous transaction wrapper
gaps.notes: better-sqlite3 synchronous .transaction() returns a callable; Turso has no equivalent — must use async batch() or manual BEGIN/COMMIT
